<!-- Generated by an 8-dimension multi-agent review (find -> 2-skeptic verify -> synthesize), 2026-06-05. Range 734dec5..9b17200. 29 raw findings, 21 confirmed, 6 contested, 2 rejected. -->

# Render-Drift Engine + Follow-ups: Code Review

The render-drift engine (`core/drift.py`, the four `render_*_page`/`existing_slugs`/`hugo_path` bindings, `mf status`, and the `<module> diff` surface) is well-architected: a clean `Renderer` protocol seam, semantic frontmatter-plus-body equality, and deterministic render functions. The headline issue is not in the drift engine itself but in the sibling series render-command hook shipped in the same range: `mf series sync --dry-run` executes the user's arbitrary `render_command` shell build, violating the repo's documented dry-run contract. Two robustness gaps round out the important tier: a single malformed on-disk page crashes every `diff`/`generate --dry-run`, and date-less papers render a static placeholder that perpetually false-flags as stale in the read-only surfaces. Nothing here is data-destroying or a hard release blocker, but the dry-run violation and the diff crash should be fixed before relying on these surfaces operationally.

## Critical

None.

## Important

- **`mf series sync --dry-run` executes the user's `render_command` shell build, mutating the source repo**
  `src/mf/series/syncer.py:434` (with `run_render_command` at 385-408; caller at `src/mf/series/commands.py:947`)
  `plan_pull_sync` calls `run_render_command(entry)` unconditionally during the planning phase, and the sync command threads `dry_run` only into `execute_sync`, never into planning. So a `--dry-run` that prints "DRY RUN - no changes will be made" still shells out via `subprocess.run(command, shell=True, cwd=source_dir)`, running the user's notebook/Makefile build and writing artifacts into the source repo; the empty-render abort guard (syncer.py:439-444) can also spuriously fire during the preview. Fix: add a `dry_run` parameter to `plan_pull_sync` and `run_render_command`, skip the subprocess (print "would run render_command: ...") and skip the empty-source abort when `dry_run` is True.

- **A single malformed on-disk `index.md` crashes `mf <module> diff` and `generate --dry-run`**
  `src/mf/core/drift.py:74-77` (`_semantic_equal` called unguarded at line 97)
  `check_render_drift` calls `parse_post(path)` (PyYAML via `frontmatter.load`) for every existing page with no try/except anywhere in `drift.py`; one hand-edited page with broken frontmatter raises `yaml.YAMLError` and aborts the entire read-only command with a traceback. These are precisely the diagnostic commands meant to surface such problems. `mf status` survives only because `collect_status` (status.py:32-35) wraps each module in a broad try/except; the per-module commands lack that. Fix: wrap the parse in try/except `(yaml.YAMLError, OSError)` and emit `RenderFinding(slug, "error", ...)` instead of propagating (`STATUS_STYLE` already has an `error` style).

- **Date-less papers render a static `2024-01-01` that diverges from on-disk dates, causing perpetual false drift**
  `src/mf/papers/generator.py:176-178, 184-186, 219-232`
  The render split removed the mtime date fallback from the read path (`render_paper_page`), so a paper with no stored/extractable date renders `date: 2024-01-01`, while its on-disk page (written by the old code) carries a real date. The durable date pin lives only in the write path (`generate_paper_content`), which the read-only `diff`/`status` paths never run, so `check_render_drift` reports those pages stale on every run with no read-only way to reconcile. Reproduced on the live site (~8 date-less, outside-`paper_db` papers affected; all 54 in-db papers carry a date so the pin never fires for them). Fix: in `generate_paper_content`, before falling back to `paper_dir` mtime, pin the date read from the existing `content/papers/{slug}/index.md` when present, so a single `generate` clears the drift without shifting reader-visible dates.

- **`projects generate --dry-run` misreports a hidden-project deletion as "skip (orphan)"**
  `src/mf/projects/commands.py:514-521` (with `drift.py:70`, `generator.py:463-477`)
  The new dry-run path routes through `print_dry_run_preview(make_renderer())`. `ProjectsRenderer.iter_slugs()` excludes hidden projects while `existing_slugs()` lists every on-disk dir, so a `hide=True` project with a leftover content dir lands in `on_disk - known`, is classified `orphan`, and `_DRY_RUN_VERB` maps it to `skip (orphan)`. But the real `generate` calls `generate_all_projects`, which `shutil.rmtree`s that dir, so the preview claims it will skip a directory it actually deletes (the old dry-run path printed "Would delete hidden"). Fix: either keep the projects dry-run on `generate_all_projects(dry_run=True)`, or teach the projects preview to emit "would delete" for a hidden-project orphan; add a test for the hidden-with-leftover-dir case.

- **`render_command` subprocess has no timeout: a hung build blocks the whole sync (and `--all` batch)**
  `src/mf/series/syncer.py:400-403`
  `subprocess.run` passes no `timeout`, so a render that deadlocks, waits on the network, or hits an interactive prompt blocks `plan_pull_sync` forever. The per-series `except RuntimeError` at `commands.py:948` cannot help because a hang never raises, so `mf series sync --all` stalls on the first stuck series with no clean interruption. Fix: add `timeout=` (e.g. 600), catch `subprocess.TimeoutExpired`, and re-raise as `RuntimeError` so the existing per-series handler reports it and continues; consider making the timeout a series field.

- **Papers date-pin durability (the central claim of the date-pin work) is never tested across a reload**
  `tests/test_papers/test_drift.py:49-61`
  `test_papers_drift_missing_then_current` generates then checks drift using the *same* in-memory `PaperDatabase` that `db.update()` already mutated, so the test passes even if `db.save()` were dropped or the pin written to a non-persisted field. The durability guarantee (code comment "save so the pin is durable across runs" and CLAUDE.md) is what `make_renderer()`'s freshly-loaded db relies on; a non-persisted pin would make `mf papers diff`/`status` fall back to the placeholder and report every such page permanently stale. Fix: add a test that generates, constructs a brand-new `PaperDatabase`, loads from disk, asserts the pinned date persisted, builds a fresh renderer, and asserts `current`; also assert a second generate does not re-pin.

## Minor

- **Projects render-drift silently under-reports a mode-switched project (both `index.md` and `_index.md` on disk)**
  `src/mf/projects/generator.py:466-489`
  `existing_slugs()` reports a slug present if either file exists and `hugo_path()` resolves only the authoritative one, so a leaf-to-branch switch leaves the stale peer file invisible to `check_render_drift` (the slug is "known", never an orphan). Read-only accuracy gap only; the limitation is acknowledged in the on-disk comment (lines 470-472). Fix: emit a synthetic orphan finding when both files exist for a slug, or document the gap in the diff help.

- **Publications write path omits `encoding='utf-8'`, the one generator that can disagree with the drift comparison**
  `src/mf/publications/generate.py:103`
  `out_file.write_text(content)` lacks an encoding while papers/projects/packages all pass `encoding='utf-8'`, and the drift reader (`parse_post` -> `frontmatter.load`) always decodes UTF-8. On a non-UTF-8 host, non-ASCII author names/titles (dumped with `allow_unicode=True`) could decode differently and report the page perpetually stale. Low impact on UTF-8 Linux. Fix: add `encoding='utf-8'` to the write.

- **`diff` takes a positional slug while `generate` takes `--slug` within papers/projects/pubs**
  `src/mf/papers/commands.py:88` vs `:63` (same split in projects 567/496, pubs 405/375)
  The new `diff` commands use a positional argument while the sibling `generate` commands use an option, so within one module a user runs `mf papers generate --slug X` but `mf papers diff X`. Packages is the only internally consistent module (both positional). Ergonomic/discoverability wart only. Fix: make the new `diff` commands match their module's `generate` selector shape.

- **`findings.py` docstring overstates the audit+integrity "shared spine" unification**
  `src/mf/core/findings.py:3-4`
  The docstring says series audit and integrity check "render summaries identically," but `integrity_commands.py` imports only `SEVERITY_STYLE` and must normalize its own `'warning'` -> `'warn'` (integrity_commands.py:112-114) because `IssueSeverity.WARNING == 'warning'` does not match the spine's `WARN = 'warn'`. The summary helpers have a single caller (`series/audit.py:272`); routing integrity findings through them would silently drop `'warning'` counts (no KeyError, `severity_counts` uses `.get`). Fix: either map `WARNING -> 'warn'` at the source and route integrity through `severity_summary`, or soften the docstring and add a `'warning' -> 'warn'` alias.

- **`run_render_command` discards success output and inherits stdin: silent warnings, possible interactive-prompt hang**
  `src/mf/series/syncer.py:400-403`
  `capture_output=True` with no `stdin` redirect means a build tool that unexpectedly reads stdin blocks invisibly (compounding the no-timeout issue), and on exit 0 both stdout and stderr are discarded so a succeeds-with-warnings build gives no signal even under verbose. Fix: pass `stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL` to fail fast on EOF, and tail captured output on success when verbose.

- **`mf series sync --dry-run` render behavior is untested**
  `tests/test_series/test_syncer.py:1486-1598`
  `TestRenderCommand` covers no-op-when-unset, cwd, non-zero-exit, empty-output-abort, and run-before-read, but no test asserts what happens under `--dry-run`, so the dry-run violation above could regress (or be fixed) uncaught. Fix: drive `mf series sync <slug> --dry-run` via `CliRunner` on a series whose `render_command` writes a sentinel and assert the intended contract (skip after the fix is applied).

- **Per-module orphan detection is never tested through the real renderer bindings**
  `tests/test_projects/test_drift.py:1-109`
  The `orphan` status is exercised only with the synthetic `FakeRenderer` in `test_core/test_drift.py`; none of the four per-module drift tests produce an orphan through the real binding, so `_is_hidden` filtering and the dual-file `existing_slugs` branch are uncovered. Fix: add a projects test that generates a page, sets `hide=True`, and asserts `orphan`; optionally mirror for papers/packages/pubs by deleting the db/artifact and leaving the page.

- **Stale-after-edit through real bindings is only tested for publications**
  `tests/test_papers/test_drift.py:49-61` (and projects/packages drift tests)
  Only `test_publications/test_drift.py::test_stale_after_title_change` exercises edit -> `stale` through a real renderer; papers/projects/packages cover only missing -> current. A render that drops a contributing field would be caught for pubs but not the other three. Fix: mirror the pubs stale test in papers, projects, and packages.

- **`print_status` aggregation (TOTAL row math and `(error)` label) is unverified**
  `tests/test_core/test_status.py:34-53`
  The dashboard's only nontrivial logic (per-status counting, `drift = stale+missing+orphan+error`, and the errored-module `(error)` label at status.py:73) is never asserted; CLI tests check only `exit_code == 0`, the static title, and one slug. Fix: call `print_status` directly with a hand-built results list (one error module) via `Console(file=StringIO(), width=200)` and assert the counts, TOTAL drift, and `(error)` label.

- **New `description` field in `posts list --json` output is not asserted**
  `tests/test_posts/test_commands.py:107-116`
  Commit 72fa3e3 added `"description": it.front_matter.get("description", "")` to the JSON (commands.py:217); `test_json_output` asserts only title and slug, so dropping the key or changing the default would pass. Fix: assert `data[0]['description']` carries the front-matter value and defaults to `''` when absent.

- **`PapersRenderer` re-resolves `get_paths()` in `render_page` instead of using injected `self._paths`**
  `src/mf/papers/generator.py:157` (renderer at 323-343) (contested)
  `PapersRenderer` stores `self._paths` and uses it in `iter_slugs`/`existing_slugs`/`hugo_path`, but `render_paper_page` re-calls `get_paths()` internally, breaking the otherwise-uniform "renderer holds all the state it needs" seam. Harmless today because `get_paths()` is `lru_cache`-backed and all callers pass the default; a caller injecting a non-default `SitePaths` would get divergent path resolution. Fix: thread an optional `paths` parameter through `render_paper_page` and pass `self._paths`.

- **Projects with no GitHub `created_at` now emit null `date`/`year_started` instead of a fabricated value**
  `src/mf/projects/generator.py:137,149,189` (contested)
  The determinism refactor replaced `datetime.now()` defaults with empty-string fallbacks, so a cache entry lacking `created_at` produces bare `date:`/`year_started:` lines that YAML parses to null. Removing the wall-clock default is correct; real GitHub repos always carry `created_at`, so only date-less cache entries are affected. Fix: mirror packages/papers and only append the lines when `created_at` is truthy.

- **Package pages now omit `date:` when `date_added` is missing (was: defaulted to today)**
  `src/mf/packages/generator.py:31-33` (contested)
  The intended determinism fix; `mf packages add` always sets `date_added` (commands.py:198), so only hand-edited/legacy entries lose the field. Informational behavior change. Fix: no code change needed; backfill `date_added` on legacy entries if a Hugo layout assumes every package page has a date.

- **Cached thumbnails now always set the `image` frontmatter field (was: only on (re)generation)**
  `src/mf/papers/generator.py:184-186` (contested)
  The old code set `image` only when the thumbnail was regenerated that run, so cache-hit papers lacked it; `render_paper_page` now sets it whenever the thumbnail exists. This is a latent-bug fix (cached thumbnails should be referenced) but produces a one-time drift wave: the first `diff`/`generate` after this change rewrites every paper page with a cached thumbnail (~53 on the live site). Fix: none required if intended; confirm the Hugo paper template handles the now-present `image` field.

## Contested / low-confidence

- **`_DRY_RUN_VERB` has no `'error'` entry, would render "would error: \<path\>"**  -  `src/mf/core/drift.py:66-71, 175` (verified as a non-issue: the `'error'` status is unreachable through `print_dry_run_preview`; purely cosmetic latent gap)
- **Papers drift tests mask the migration mismatch**  -  `tests/test_papers/test_drift.py:49-61` (overlaps the date-pin durability gap above; one skeptic judged the underlying "perpetual stale" narrative overstated)
- **`render_command` shell=True threat model is undocumented**  -  `src/mf/series/syncer.py:385-408` (verified as a non-issue: provenance is local user-owned config, and the docstring already states it runs via the shell as local build config)
- **`render_command` runs before pull but not before push**  -  `src/mf/series/syncer.py:434, 544-546` (design asymmetry; one skeptic deemed it semantically correct since push overwrites the source)

## What's solid

- **The `Renderer` protocol seam is genuinely clean and uniform.** Each module binds `iter_slugs`/`existing_slugs`/`hugo_path`/`render_page`, and `check_render_drift` (drift.py:80-105) is module-agnostic. The four-branch classification (current/stale/missing/orphan) is correctly non-double-counted: the second loop uses `on_disk - known`, disjoint from the first.
- **Semantic equality is done right.** `_semantic_equal` (drift.py:74-77) compares parsed frontmatter dicts plus body, never textual YAML, so key-order or quoting differences don't produce false drift. The shared `parse_post`/`parse_text`/`frontmatter_equal` primitives in `core/frontmatter.py` are the correct abstraction.
- **Render determinism is deliberately enforced.** The render functions are documented and built to avoid wall-clock dates (`render_paper_page` comment at generator.py:153/176-178; the projects/packages refactors removing `datetime.now()`), and the papers date pin keeps a stored date stable across runs. This is the right design even though the read-path pin gap (important finding above) is a wrinkle in its execution.
- **`generate --dry-run` and `<module> diff` share one engine.** `print_dry_run_preview` and `run_diff_command` both route through `check_render_drift`, so the preview and the read-only diff cannot diverge in their drift logic.
- **The series render hook was hardened defensively.** `run_render_command` raises on non-zero exit, guards `source_dir is None`, and includes an explicit empty-render abort (syncer.py:439-444) that prevents an empty build from driving a mass REMOVE. The remaining gaps (dry-run, timeout, stdin) are refinements on an already-careful path.
- **`mf status` degrades gracefully on module failure.** `collect_status` (status.py:32-35) wraps each module in try/except and synthesizes an `error` finding, and `STATUS_STYLE` has a matching style. This is the pattern the per-module `diff` commands should adopt.

## Recommended next actions

1. Thread `dry_run` into `plan_pull_sync`/`run_render_command` so `mf series sync --dry-run` does not execute the build (and skip the empty-source abort under dry-run); add the missing CLI dry-run test.
2. Wrap the on-disk parse in `check_render_drift`/`_semantic_equal` in try/except and emit an `error` finding instead of crashing `diff`/`generate --dry-run`.
3. Fix the papers date pin to reconcile to the existing page date; add the pin-then-reload durability test.
4. Make `projects generate --dry-run` report hidden-project deletions as "would delete" rather than "skip (orphan)"; add the hidden-with-leftover-dir test.
5. Add a `timeout` (and `stdin=DEVNULL`) to the `render_command` subprocess, re-raising `TimeoutExpired` as `RuntimeError`.
6. Add `encoding='utf-8'` to the publications write; unify the `diff` slug selector with each module's `generate`.
7. Close the remaining test gaps: real-binding orphan detection, edit->stale for papers/projects/packages, `print_status` aggregation, and the posts `--json` description field.
8. Soften the `findings.py` docstring (or add the `'warning' -> 'warn'` alias); be aware the first papers regenerate after this range rewrites every page with a cached thumbnail (expected, one-time).
